<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:村国氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:村国氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:村国氏]]
'''Murakuni Clan''' ({{ill|Murakuni Clan|ja|村国氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Clan|qq=}}, Murakuni-ji) is a family that flourished as regional magnates and central officials in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} from the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} to the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. Their hereditary title ({{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane|1=Kabane}}) is {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Ren|1=Muraji}}. Their stronghold was in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}, {{ill|Kagami District|ja|各務郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami District|1=Kakami district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412369}}, and after {{ill|Murakuni Oyori|en|Murakuni Oyori|WD=Q6937264|lt=Murakuni Otokoroe|1=Murakuni Oyori}} distinguished himself during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}} in {{ill|672|en|672|WD=Q30601|lt=672|1=672}}, they went on to produce mid-level officials. In the 760s they were patronized by {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}, but this association led to their decline after the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|WD=Q11623017|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion}} in {{ill|764|en|764|WD=Q31163|lt=764|1=764}}.
== The Murakuni Clan in Mino Province == The Murakuni Clan was based in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}’s {{ill|Kagami District|ja|各務郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami District|1=Kakami district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412369}}, and there is a theory that they were a branch of the family of the {{ill|Owari Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|WD=Q11465294}} ({{ill|Owari Ren|ja|尾張連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Ren|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Little is directly known about the Murakuni Clan prior to Otokoroe, and on a national scale they seem to have been an almost obscure minor local clan.
In the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} work ''{{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|en|Wamyō Ruijushō|WD=Q2702542|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}'', Murakuni communities (郷) are recorded in three locations: in {{ill|Kagami District, Mino Province|ja|美濃国各務郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami District, Mino Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province|1=Owari Province}}’s {{ill|Haguri District|en|Haguri District|WD=Q1032507|lt=Haguri District|1=Haguri District}}, and in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}’s {{ill|Soge District|ja|添下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soge District|1=Soejimo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560415}}. It is speculated that the Murakuni-gō in Mino and Owari, which are separated only by the {{ill|Kiso River|en|Kiso River|WD=Q779607|lt=Kiso River|1=Kiso River}}, originally referred to the same area. The Heian-era "Engishiki Jinmyōchō" records that there are two Murakuni Shrines ({{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shrine|1=Murakuni Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523055}}) and one {{ill|Murakuni Mamusuda Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Mamusuda Shrine|1=Murakuni Masumida Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523053}} in Kagami District. In Haguri District, although no Murakuni Shrine appears in the ''Engishiki'', there is another shrine bearing the name, and the temple {{ill|Ongaku-ji (Konan)|ja|音楽寺 (江南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ongaku-ji (Konan)|1=Ongaku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664255}} carries the tradition of having been built by Murakuni Otokoroe.
Furthermore, as noted later, Otokoroe and his grandson, {{ill|Murakuni Shimasu|ja|村国島主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shimasu|1=Q11523049|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523049}}, had connections with Mino. In the 2nd year of the {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|WD=Q423370|lt=Daihō era|1=Taiho}} ({{ill|702|en|702|WD=Q23497|lt=702|1=702}}), a woman named Murakuni Okuren Kobiryū (奥連小竜女) appears in the family registers of the 【御野国山方郡三井田】. The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'' records that in the 4th year of the {{ill|Keiun|en|Keiun|WD=Q428753|lt=Keiun era|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|AD 707|en|AD 707|WD=Q23510|lt=707|1=AD 707}}) on May 16, a certain Murakuni Rento-shimē (村国連等志売) of Mino Province bore triplets. In this way, matters and persons associated with the Murakuni Clan are concentrated in Mino Province, lending strong support to the theory linking the Murakuni Clan with Kagami District, Mino.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' attributes the surname {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Ren|1=Muraji}} to Murakuni Otokoroe at the time of the Jinshin War, and subsequent historical texts consistently record the Murakuni Clan under the Ren surname. However, the Ren surname in the era of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}—in the period immediately before and after the establishment of the "Eight Colors of Surnames" ({{ill|Yashoku no Uji|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashoku no Uji|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}})—differed greatly in status, and the Ren surname at the time of the Jinshin War would have been too exalted for a minor local clan. An article in the 1st year of the {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|WD=Q423370|lt=Daihō era|1=Taiho}} ({{ill|701|en|701|WD=Q23496|lt=701|1=701}}) in July, which lists heroes of the Jinshin War along with their surnames, records Murakuni Otokoroe (referred to as Murakuni Koyori (小依)) without any surname. If this is given due weight, it suggests that the clan did not originally possess a surname at the time of the Jinshin War, and that the Ren surname was conferred upon them afterward.
== The Murakuni Clan in the Imperial Court == The Murakuni Clan is introduced in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' through Murakuni Otokoroe, who served {{ill|Prince Ōama (later Emperor Tenmu)|ja|大海人皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama (later Emperor Tenmu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ({{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}). In the year of the Jinshin War ({{ill|672|en|672|WD=Q30601|lt=672|1=672}}), in June, Otokoroe was dispatched as an envoy to Mino Province to deliver Prince Ōama's order to raise an army—likely a consideration of his Mino origins. In July, he served as a central commander of the forces in the Omi region, which became the main theater of operations. Following the Jinshin War, Otokoroe was granted a fudo (封戸). After his death, he was awarded the Rank of external {{ill|Koshiki|ja|小紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshiki|1=Shōshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463467}}, and his son, {{ill|Murakuni Shigamaro|ja|村国志我麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shigamaro|1=Murakuni no Shigamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523052}}, was granted a honor known as {{ill|Kōden|ja|功田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōden|1=kouden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398438}}. Although these honors may seem modest compared to those given to the greatest achievers, they were significant rewards for someone originating from a local magnate.
In the first half of the 8th century, the Murakuni Clan attained mid- to lower-level noble status; Shigamaro reached the Rank of {{ill|Ju-go-i Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ju-go-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, while {{ill|Murakuni Komushi|ja|村国子虫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Komushi|1=Q11523050|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523050}} attained the Rank of external {{ill|Ju-go-i Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ju-go-i Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, barely reaching the 5th Rank despite his external status.
At the height of {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}’s power, Otokoroe’s grandson, {{ill|Murakuni Shimasu|ja|村国島主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shimasu|1=Q11523049|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523049}}, initially entered Nakamaro’s service, and the clan enjoyed his patronage. Just before the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|WD=Q11623017|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion}} in the 8th year of the {{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|WD=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} ({{ill|764|en|764|WD=Q31163|lt=764|1=764}}), Shimasu held the position of {{ill|Shōyaku|ja|少掾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōyaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 掾|1=jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65269195}} in Mino, {{ill|Murakuni Koro|ja|村国子老|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Koro|1=Q11523048|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523048}} served as the governor ({{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|WD=Q1351234|lt=Kokushi|1=Kokushi (official)}}) of {{ill|Noto Province|en|Noto Province|WD=Q1047671|lt=Noto Province|1=Noto Province}}, and {{ill|Murakuni Mushi-maro|ja|村国虫麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Mushi-maro|1=Q11523056|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523056}} held the post of secretary ({{ill|Kaisei|ja|介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国司|1=Kokushi (official)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1351234}}) in {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province|1=Echizen Province}}. This distribution of posts appears to have been part of Nakamaro’s plan to mobilize military forces from eastern and northern regions centered on {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Omi Province|1=Ōmi Province}}, but his failure to raise an army and subsequent defeat meant that the plan never came to fruition. Shimasu was killed by an envoy sent to secure the fortification at {{ill|Fuwa Barrier|ja|不破関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwa Barrier|1=Fuwa no Seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10868912}}, and the other two lost their gubernatorial appointments. Later, the Imperial Court exonerated Murakuni Shimasu. Although Koro and Mushi-maro, who had once fallen from power, later returned to serve at the same Rank as before, their descendants were unable to maintain their status.
Much later, a woman named {{ill|Murakuni Kazuko|ja|村国数子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Kazuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who worked in the Imperial harem, reached the Rank of external {{ill|Ju-go-i Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ju-go-i Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in the 17th year of the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan|1=Zhenguan}} era ({{ill|875|en|875|WD=Q23816|lt=875|1=875}}); she was the last member of the Murakuni Clan to attain the 5th Rank. Subsequently, the names of {{ill|Murakuni Gyōse|ja|村国業世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Gyōse|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (of the upper {{ill|Shōroku|ja|正六位|1=Senior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=}}) and his son {{ill|Murakuni Harusawa|ja|村国春沢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Harusawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} appear in the "Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku" in the 4th clause of the 1st year of the {{ill|Ninna|en|Ninna|WD=Q1346317|lt=Ninwa|1=Ninna}} era ({{ill|885|en|885|WD=Q24894|lt=885|1=885}}).

'''Murakuni Clan''' ({{ill|Murakuni Clan|ja|村国氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Clan|qq=}}, Murakuni-ji) is a family that flourished as regional magnates and central officials in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} from the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} to the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. Their hereditary title ({{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane|1=Kabane}}) is {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Ren|1=Muraji}}. Their stronghold was in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}, {{ill|Kagami District|ja|各務郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami District|1=Kakami district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412369}}, and after {{ill|Murakuni Oyori|en|Murakuni Oyori|WD=Q6937264|lt=Murakuni Otokoroe|1=Murakuni Oyori}} distinguished himself during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}} in {{ill|672|en|672|WD=Q30601|lt=672|1=672}}, they went on to produce mid-level officials. In the 760s they were patronized by {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}, but this association led to their decline after the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|WD=Q11623017|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion}} in {{ill|764|en|764|WD=Q31163|lt=764|1=764}}.
== The Murakuni Clan in Mino Province == The Murakuni Clan was based in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}’s {{ill|Kagami District|ja|各務郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami District|1=Kakami district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412369}}, and there is a theory that they were a branch of the family of the {{ill|Owari Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|WD=Q11465294}} ({{ill|Owari Ren|ja|尾張連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Ren|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Little is directly known about the Murakuni Clan prior to Otokoroe, and on a national scale they seem to have been an almost obscure minor local clan.
In the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} work ''{{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|en|Wamyō Ruijushō|WD=Q2702542|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}'', Murakuni communities (郷) are recorded in three locations: in {{ill|Kagami District, Mino Province|ja|美濃国各務郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami District, Mino Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province|1=Owari Province}}’s {{ill|Haguri District|en|Haguri District|WD=Q1032507|lt=Haguri District|1=Haguri District}}, and in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}’s {{ill|Soge District|ja|添下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soge District|1=Soejimo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11560415}}. It is speculated that the Murakuni-gō in Mino and Owari, which are separated only by the {{ill|Kiso River|en|Kiso River|WD=Q779607|lt=Kiso River|1=Kiso River}}, originally referred to the same area. The Heian-era "Engishiki Jinmyōchō" records that there are two Murakuni Shrines ({{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shrine|1=Murakuni Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523055}}) and one {{ill|Murakuni Mamusuda Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Mamusuda Shrine|1=Murakuni Masumida Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523053}} in Kagami District. In Haguri District, although no Murakuni Shrine appears in the ''Engishiki'', there is another shrine bearing the name, and the temple {{ill|Ongaku-ji (Konan)|ja|音楽寺 (江南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ongaku-ji (Konan)|1=Ongaku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11664255}} carries the tradition of having been built by Murakuni Otokoroe.
Furthermore, as noted later, Otokoroe and his grandson, {{ill|Murakuni Shimasu|ja|村国島主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shimasu|1=Q11523049|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523049}}, had connections with Mino. In the 2nd year of the {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|WD=Q423370|lt=Daihō era|1=Taiho}} ({{ill|702|en|702|WD=Q23497|lt=702|1=702}}), a woman named Murakuni Okuren Kobiryū (奥連小竜女) appears in the family registers of the 【御野国山方郡三井田】. The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'' records that in the 4th year of the {{ill|Keiun|en|Keiun|WD=Q428753|lt=Keiun era|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|AD 707|en|AD 707|WD=Q23510|lt=707|1=AD 707}}) on May 16, a certain Murakuni Rento-shimē (村国連等志売) of Mino Province bore triplets. In this way, matters and persons associated with the Murakuni Clan are concentrated in Mino Province, lending strong support to the theory linking the Murakuni Clan with Kagami District, Mino.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' attributes the surname {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Ren|1=Muraji}} to Murakuni Otokoroe at the time of the Jinshin War, and subsequent historical texts consistently record the Murakuni Clan under the Ren surname. However, the Ren surname in the era of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}—in the period immediately before and after the establishment of the "Eight Colors of Surnames" ({{ill|Yashoku no Uji|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashoku no Uji|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}})—differed greatly in status, and the Ren surname at the time of the Jinshin War would have been too exalted for a minor local clan. An article in the 1st year of the {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|WD=Q423370|lt=Daihō era|1=Taiho}} ({{ill|701|en|701|WD=Q23496|lt=701|1=701}}) in July, which lists heroes of the Jinshin War along with their surnames, records Murakuni Otokoroe (referred to as Murakuni Koyori (小依)) without any surname. If this is given due weight, it suggests that the clan did not originally possess a surname at the time of the Jinshin War, and that the Ren surname was conferred upon them afterward.
== The Murakuni Clan in the Imperial Court == The Murakuni Clan is introduced in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' through Murakuni Otokoroe, who served {{ill|Prince Ōama (later Emperor Tenmu)|ja|大海人皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama (later Emperor Tenmu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} ({{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}). In the year of the Jinshin War ({{ill|672|en|672|WD=Q30601|lt=672|1=672}}), in June, Otokoroe was dispatched as an envoy to Mino Province to deliver Prince Ōama's order to raise an army—likely a consideration of his Mino origins. In July, he served as a central commander of the forces in the Omi region, which became the main theater of operations. Following the Jinshin War, Otokoroe was granted a fudo (封戸). After his death, he was awarded the Rank of external {{ill|Koshiki|ja|小紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshiki|1=Shōshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463467}}, and his son, {{ill|Murakuni Shigamaro|ja|村国志我麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shigamaro|1=Murakuni no Shigamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523052}}, was granted a honor known as {{ill|Kōden|ja|功田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōden|1=kouden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398438}}. Although these honors may seem modest compared to those given to the greatest achievers, they were significant rewards for someone originating from a local magnate.
In the first half of the 8th century, the Murakuni Clan attained mid- to lower-level noble status; Shigamaro reached the Rank of {{ill|Ju-go-i Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ju-go-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, while {{ill|Murakuni Komushi|ja|村国子虫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Komushi|1=Q11523050|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523050}} attained the Rank of external {{ill|Ju-go-i Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ju-go-i Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, barely reaching the 5th Rank despite his external status.
At the height of {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}’s power, Otokoroe’s grandson, {{ill|Murakuni Shimasu|ja|村国島主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shimasu|1=Q11523049|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523049}}, initially entered Nakamaro’s service, and the clan enjoyed his patronage. Just before the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|WD=Q11623017|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion}} in the 8th year of the {{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|WD=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} ({{ill|764|en|764|WD=Q31163|lt=764|1=764}}), Shimasu held the position of {{ill|Shōyaku|ja|少掾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōyaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 掾|1=jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65269195}} in Mino, {{ill|Murakuni Koro|ja|村国子老|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Koro|1=Q11523048|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523048}} served as the governor ({{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|WD=Q1351234|lt=Kokushi|1=Kokushi (official)}}) of {{ill|Noto Province|en|Noto Province|WD=Q1047671|lt=Noto Province|1=Noto Province}}, and {{ill|Murakuni Mushi-maro|ja|村国虫麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Mushi-maro|1=Q11523056|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523056}} held the post of secretary ({{ill|Kaisei|ja|介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国司|1=Kokushi (official)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1351234}}) in {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province|1=Echizen Province}}. This distribution of posts appears to have been part of Nakamaro’s plan to mobilize military forces from eastern and northern regions centered on {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Omi Province|1=Ōmi Province}}, but his failure to raise an army and subsequent defeat meant that the plan never came to fruition. Shimasu was killed by an envoy sent to secure the fortification at {{ill|Fuwa Barrier|ja|不破関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwa Barrier|1=Fuwa no Seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10868912}}, and the other two lost their gubernatorial appointments. Later, the Imperial Court exonerated Murakuni Shimasu. Although Koro and Mushi-maro, who had once fallen from power, later returned to serve at the same Rank as before, their descendants were unable to maintain their status.
Much later, a woman named {{ill|Murakuni Kazuko|ja|村国数子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Kazuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who worked in the Imperial harem, reached the Rank of external {{ill|Ju-go-i Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ju-go-i Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in the 17th year of the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan|1=Zhenguan}} era ({{ill|875|en|875|WD=Q23816|lt=875|1=875}}); she was the last member of the Murakuni Clan to attain the 5th Rank. Subsequently, the names of {{ill|Murakuni Gyōse|ja|村国業世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Gyōse|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (of the upper {{ill|Shōroku|ja|正六位|1=Senior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=}}) and his son {{ill|Murakuni Harusawa|ja|村国春沢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Harusawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} appear in the "Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku" in the 4th clause of the 1st year of the {{ill|Ninna|en|Ninna|WD=Q1346317|lt=Ninwa|1=Ninna}} era ({{ill|885|en|885|WD=Q24894|lt=885|1=885}}).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:村国氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:村国氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:村国氏]]
'''Murakuni Clan''' ({{ill|Murakuni Clan|ja|村国氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Clan|qq=}}, Murakuni-ji) is a family that flourished as regional magnates and central officials in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} from the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} to the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. Their hereditary title ({{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane|1=Kabane}}) is {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|WD=Q2735611|lt=Ren|1=Muraji}}. Their stronghold was in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}, {{ill|Kagami District|ja|各務郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami District|1=Kakami district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412369}}, and after {{ill|Murakuni Oyori|en|Murakuni Oyori|WD=Q6937264|lt=Murakuni Otokoroe|1=Murakuni Oyori}} distinguished himself during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}} in {{ill|672|en|672|WD=Q30601|lt=672|1=672}}, they went on to produce mid-level officials. In the 760s they were patronized by {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}, but this association led to their decline after the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|WD=Q11623017|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion}} in {{ill|764|en|764|WD=Q31163|lt=764|1=764}}.
== The Murakuni Clan in Mino Province == The Murakuni Clan was based in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}’s {{ill|Kagami District|ja|各務郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami District|1=Kakami district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11412369}}, and there is a theory that they were a branch of the family of the {{ill|Owari Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|WD=Q11465294}} ({{ill|Owari Ren|ja|尾張連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Ren|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Furthermore, as noted later, Otokoroe and his grandson, {{ill|Murakuni Shimasu|ja|村国島主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shimasu|1=Q11523049|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523049}}, had connections with Mino. In this way, matters and persons associated with the Murakuni Clan are concentrated in Mino Province, lending strong support to the theory linking the Murakuni Clan with Kagami District, Mino.

After his death, he was awarded the Rank of external {{ill|Koshiki|ja|小紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshiki|1=Shōshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11463467}}, and his son, {{ill|Murakuni Shigamaro|ja|村国志我麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shigamaro|1=Murakuni no Shigamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523052}}, was granted a honor known as {{ill|Kōden|ja|功田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōden|1=kouden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11398438}}. Although these honors may seem modest compared to those given to the greatest achievers, they were significant rewards for someone originating from a local magnate.
In the first half of the 8th century, the Murakuni Clan attained mid- to lower-level noble status; Shigamaro reached the Rank of {{ill|Ju-go-i Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ju-go-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, while {{ill|Murakuni Komushi|ja|村国子虫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Komushi|1=Q11523050|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523050}} attained the Rank of external {{ill|Ju-go-i Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ju-go-i Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, barely reaching the 5th Rank despite his external status.
At the height of {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|WD=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}’s power, Otokoroe’s grandson, {{ill|Murakuni Shimasu|ja|村国島主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shimasu|1=Q11523049|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523049}}, initially entered Nakamaro’s service, and the clan enjoyed his patronage. Just before the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|WD=Q11623017|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion}} in the 8th year of the {{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|WD=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} ({{ill|764|en|764|WD=Q31163|lt=764|1=764}}), Shimasu held the position of {{ill|Shōyaku|ja|少掾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōyaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 掾|1=jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65269195}} in Mino, {{ill|Murakuni Koro|ja|村国子老|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Koro|1=Q11523048|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523048}} served as the governor ({{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|WD=Q1351234|lt=Kokushi|1=Kokushi (official)}}) of {{ill|Noto Province|en|Noto Province|WD=Q1047671|lt=Noto Province|1=Noto Province}}, and {{ill|Murakuni Mushi-maro|ja|村国虫麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Mushi-maro|1=Q11523056|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523056}} held the post of secretary ({{ill|Kaisei|ja|介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国司|1=Kokushi (official)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1351234}}) in {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province|1=Echizen Province}}. This distribution of posts appears to have been part of Nakamaro’s plan to mobilize military forces from eastern and northern regions centered on {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}}, but his failure to raise an army and subsequent defeat meant that the plan never came to fruition. Shimasu was killed by an envoy sent to secure the fortification at {{ill|Fuwa Barrier|ja|不破関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwa Barrier|1=Fuwa no Seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10868912}}, and the other two lost their gubernatorial appointments. Later, the Imperial Court exonerated Murakuni Shimasu. Although Koro and Mushi-maro, who had once fallen from power, later returned to serve at the same Rank as before, their descendants were unable to maintain their status.
Much later, a woman named {{ill|Murakuni Kazuko|ja|村国数子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Kazuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who worked in the Imperial harem, reached the Rank of external {{ill|Ju-go-i Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ju-go-i Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in the 17th year of the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan|1=Zhenguan}} era ({{ill|875|en|875|WD=Q23816|lt=875|1=875}}); she was the last member of the Murakuni Clan to attain the 5th Rank. Subsequently, the names of {{ill|Murakuni Gyōse|ja|村国業世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Gyōse|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (of the upper {{ill|Shōroku|ja|正六位|1=Senior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=}}) and his son {{ill|Murakuni Harusawa|ja|村国春沢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Harusawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} appear in the "Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku" in the 4th clause of the 1st year of the {{ill|Ninna|en|Ninna|WD=Q1346317|lt=Ninwa|1=Ninna}} era ({{ill|885|en|885|WD=Q24894|lt=885|1=885}}).
==See Also== * {{ill|Tejikaraō Shrine (Kakamigahara)|ja|手力雄神社 (各務原市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tejikaraō Shrine (Kakamigahara)|qq=}}

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ja:村国氏]] {{wikidata link|Q11523054}} [[Category:Clans of Chūbu Region]] [[Category:Clans of Mino Province]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Murakuni Clan]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:村国氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:村国氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:村国氏]]
'''Murakuni Clan''' ({{ill|Murakuni Clan|ja|村国氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Clan|qq=}}, Murakuni-ji) is a family that flourished as regional magnates and central officials in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} from the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} to the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. Their hereditary title ({{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|qid=Q1664099|lt=Kabane|1=Kabane}}) is {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|qid=Q2735611|lt=Ren|1=Muraji}}. Their stronghold was in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|qid=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}, {{ill|Kagami District|ja|各務郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami District|1=Kakami district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11412369}}, and after {{ill|Murakuni Oyori|en|Murakuni Oyori|qid=Q6937264|lt=Murakuni Otokoroe|1=Murakuni Oyori}} distinguished himself during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|qid=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}} in {{ill|672|en|672|qid=Q30601|lt=672|1=672}}, they went on to produce mid-level officials. In the 760s they were patronized by {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|qid=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}, but this association led to their decline after the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|qid=Q11623017|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion}} in {{ill|764|en|764|qid=Q31163|lt=764|1=764}}.
== The Murakuni Clan in Mino Province == The Murakuni Clan was based in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|qid=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}’s {{ill|Kagami District|ja|各務郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami District|1=Kakami district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11412369}}, and there is a theory that they were a branch of the family of the {{ill|Owari Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qid=Q11465294}} ({{ill|Owari Ren|ja|尾張連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Ren|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Little is directly known about the Murakuni Clan prior to Otokoroe, and on a national scale they seem to have been an almost obscure minor local clan.
In the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} work ''{{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|en|Wamyō Ruijushō|qid=Q2702542|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}'', Murakuni communities (郷) are recorded in three locations: in {{ill|Kagami District, Mino Province|ja|美濃国各務郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami District, Mino Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|qid=Q514506|lt=Owari Province|1=Owari Province}}’s {{ill|Haguri District|en|Haguri District|qid=Q1032507|lt=Haguri District|1=Haguri District}}, and in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}’s {{ill|Soge District|ja|添下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soge District|1=Soejimo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11560415}}. It is speculated that the Murakuni-gō in Mino and Owari, which are separated only by the {{ill|Kiso River|en|Kiso River|qid=Q779607|lt=Kiso River|1=Kiso River}}, originally referred to the same area. The Heian-era "Engishiki Jinmyōchō" records that there are two Murakuni Shrines ({{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shrine|1=Murakuni Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11523055}}) and one {{ill|Murakuni Mamusuda Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Mamusuda Shrine|1=Murakuni Masumida Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11523053}} in Kagami District. In Haguri District, although no Murakuni Shrine appears in the ''Engishiki'', there is another shrine bearing the name, and the temple {{ill|Ongaku-ji (Konan)|ja|音楽寺 (江南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ongaku-ji (Konan)|1=Ongaku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11664255}} carries the tradition of having been built by Murakuni Otokoroe.
Furthermore, as noted later, Otokoroe and his grandson, {{ill|Murakuni Shimasu|ja|村国島主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shimasu|1=Q11523049|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11523049}}, had connections with Mino. In the 2nd year of the {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|qid=Q423370|lt=Daihō era|1=Taiho}} ({{ill|702|en|702|qid=Q23497|lt=702|1=702}}), a woman named Murakuni Okuren Kobiryū (奥連小竜女) appears in the family registers of the 【御野国山方郡三井田】. The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|qid=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'' records that in the 4th year of the {{ill|Keiun|en|Keiun|qid=Q428753|lt=Keiun era|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|AD 707|en|AD 707|qid=Q23510|lt=707|1=AD 707}}) on May 16, a certain Murakuni Rento-shimē (村国連等志売) of Mino Province bore triplets. In this way, matters and persons associated with the Murakuni Clan are concentrated in Mino Province, lending strong support to the theory linking the Murakuni Clan with Kagami District, Mino.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' attributes the surname {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|qid=Q2735611|lt=Ren|1=Muraji}} to Murakuni Otokoroe at the time of the Jinshin War, and subsequent historical texts consistently record the Murakuni Clan under the Ren surname. However, the Ren surname in the era of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}}—in the period immediately before and after the establishment of the "Eight Colors of Surnames" ({{ill|Yashoku no Uji|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashoku no Uji|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}})—differed greatly in status, and the Ren surname at the time of the Jinshin War would have been too exalted for a minor local clan. An article in the 1st year of the {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|qid=Q423370|lt=Daihō era|1=Taiho}} ({{ill|701|en|701|qid=Q23496|lt=701|1=701}}) in July, which lists heroes of the Jinshin War along with their surnames, records Murakuni Otokoroe (referred to as Murakuni Koyori (小依)) without any surname. If this is given due weight, it suggests that the clan did not originally possess a surname at the time of the Jinshin War, and that the Ren surname was conferred upon them afterward.
== The Murakuni Clan in the Imperial Court == The Murakuni Clan is introduced in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' through Murakuni Otokoroe, who served {{ill|Prince Ōama (later Emperor Tenmu)|ja|大海人皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama (later Emperor Tenmu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}} ({{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}}). In the year of the Jinshin War ({{ill|672|en|672|qid=Q30601|lt=672|1=672}}), in June, Otokoroe was dispatched as an envoy to Mino Province to deliver Prince Ōama's order to raise an army—likely a consideration of his Mino origins. In July, he served as a central commander of the forces in the Omi region, which became the main theater of operations. Following the Jinshin War, Otokoroe was granted a fudo (封戸). After his death, he was awarded the Rank of external {{ill|Koshiki|ja|小紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshiki|1=Shōshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11463467}}, and his son, {{ill|Murakuni Shigamaro|ja|村国志我麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shigamaro|1=Murakuni no Shigamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11523052}}, was granted a honor known as {{ill|Kōden|ja|功田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōden|1=kouden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11398438}}. Although these honors may seem modest compared to those given to the greatest achievers, they were significant rewards for someone originating from a local magnate.
In the first half of the 8th century, the Murakuni Clan attained mid- to lower-level noble status; Shigamaro reached the Rank of {{ill|Ju-go-i Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ju-go-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, while {{ill|Murakuni Komushi|ja|村国子虫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Komushi|1=Q11523050|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11523050}} attained the Rank of external {{ill|Ju-go-i Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ju-go-i Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, barely reaching the 5th Rank despite his external status.
At the height of {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|qid=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}’s power, Otokoroe’s grandson, {{ill|Murakuni Shimasu|ja|村国島主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shimasu|1=Q11523049|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11523049}}, initially entered Nakamaro’s service, and the clan enjoyed his patronage. Just before the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|qid=Q11623017|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion}} in the 8th year of the {{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|qid=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} ({{ill|764|en|764|qid=Q31163|lt=764|1=764}}), Shimasu held the position of {{ill|Shōyaku|ja|少掾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōyaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 掾|1=jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65269195}} in Mino, {{ill|Murakuni Koro|ja|村国子老|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Koro|1=Q11523048|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11523048}} served as the governor ({{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|qid=Q1351234|lt=Kokushi|1=Kokushi (official)}}) of {{ill|Noto Province|en|Noto Province|qid=Q1047671|lt=Noto Province|1=Noto Province}}, and {{ill|Murakuni Mushi-maro|ja|村国虫麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Mushi-maro|1=Q11523056|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11523056}} held the post of secretary ({{ill|Kaisei|ja|介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国司|1=Kokushi (official)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1351234}}) in {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|qid=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province|1=Echizen Province}}. This distribution of posts appears to have been part of Nakamaro’s plan to mobilize military forces from eastern and northern regions centered on {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|qid=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}}, but his failure to raise an army and subsequent defeat meant that the plan never came to fruition. Shimasu was killed by an envoy sent to secure the fortification at {{ill|Fuwa Barrier|ja|不破関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwa Barrier|1=Fuwa no Seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10868912}}, and the other two lost their gubernatorial appointments. Later, the Imperial Court exonerated Murakuni Shimasu. Although Koro and Mushi-maro, who had once fallen from power, later returned to serve at the same Rank as before, their descendants were unable to maintain their status.
Much later, a woman named {{ill|Murakuni Kazuko|ja|村国数子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Kazuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who worked in the Imperial harem, reached the Rank of external {{ill|Ju-go-i Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ju-go-i Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in the 17th year of the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|qid=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan|1=Zhenguan}} era ({{ill|875|en|875|qid=Q23816|lt=875|1=875}}); she was the last member of the Murakuni Clan to attain the 5th Rank. Subsequently, the names of {{ill|Murakuni Gyōse|ja|村国業世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Gyōse|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (of the upper {{ill|Shōroku|ja|正六位|1=Senior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=}}) and his son {{ill|Murakuni Harusawa|ja|村国春沢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Harusawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} appear in the "Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku" in the 4th clause of the 1st year of the {{ill|Ninna|en|Ninna|qid=Q1346317|lt=Ninwa|1=Ninna}} era ({{ill|885|en|885|qid=Q24894|lt=885|1=885}}).

revision.text:
'''Murakuni Clan''' ({{ill|Murakuni Clan|ja|村国氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Clan|qq=}}, Murakuni-ji) is a family that flourished as regional magnates and central officials in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} from the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} to the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}}. Their hereditary title ({{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|qid=Q1664099|lt=Kabane|1=Kabane}}) is {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|qid=Q2735611|lt=Ren|1=Muraji}}. Their stronghold was in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|qid=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}, {{ill|Kagami District|ja|各務郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami District|1=Kakami district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11412369}}, and after {{ill|Murakuni Oyori|en|Murakuni Oyori|qid=Q6937264|lt=Murakuni Otokoroe|1=Murakuni Oyori}} distinguished himself during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|qid=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}} in {{ill|672|en|672|qid=Q30601|lt=672|1=672}}, they went on to produce mid-level officials. In the 760s they were patronized by {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|qid=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}, but this association led to their decline after the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|qid=Q11623017|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion}} in {{ill|764|en|764|qid=Q31163|lt=764|1=764}}.
== The Murakuni Clan in Mino Province == The Murakuni Clan was based in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|qid=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}’s {{ill|Kagami District|ja|各務郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami District|1=Kakami district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11412369}}, and there is a theory that they were a branch of the family of the {{ill|Owari Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|尾張国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Owari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qid=Q11465294}} ({{ill|Owari Ren|ja|尾張連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari Ren|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Little is directly known about the Murakuni Clan prior to Otokoroe, and on a national scale they seem to have been an almost obscure minor local clan.
In the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}} work ''{{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|en|Wamyō Ruijushō|qid=Q2702542|lt=Wamyō Ruijushō|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}'', Murakuni communities (郷) are recorded in three locations: in {{ill|Kagami District, Mino Province|ja|美濃国各務郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagami District, Mino Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, in {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|qid=Q514506|lt=Owari Province|1=Owari Province}}’s {{ill|Haguri District|en|Haguri District|qid=Q1032507|lt=Haguri District|1=Haguri District}}, and in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}’s {{ill|Soge District|ja|添下郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soge District|1=Soejimo district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11560415}}. It is speculated that the Murakuni-gō in Mino and Owari, which are separated only by the {{ill|Kiso River|en|Kiso River|qid=Q779607|lt=Kiso River|1=Kiso River}}, originally referred to the same area. The Heian-era "Engishiki Jinmyōchō" records that there are two Murakuni Shrines ({{ill|Murakuni Shrine|ja|村国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shrine|1=Murakuni Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11523055}}) and one {{ill|Murakuni Mamusuda Shrine|ja|村国真墨田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Mamusuda Shrine|1=Murakuni Masumida Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11523053}} in Kagami District. In Haguri District, although no Murakuni Shrine appears in the ''Engishiki'', there is another shrine bearing the name, and the temple {{ill|Ongaku-ji (Konan)|ja|音楽寺 (江南市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ongaku-ji (Konan)|1=Ongaku-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11664255}} carries the tradition of having been built by Murakuni Otokoroe.
Furthermore, as noted later, Otokoroe and his grandson, {{ill|Murakuni Shimasu|ja|村国島主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shimasu|1=Q11523049|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11523049}}, had connections with Mino. In the 2nd year of the {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|qid=Q423370|lt=Daihō era|1=Taiho}} ({{ill|702|en|702|qid=Q23497|lt=702|1=702}}), a woman named Murakuni Okuren Kobiryū (奥連小竜女) appears in the family registers of the 【御野国山方郡三井田】. The ''{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|qid=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}'' records that in the 4th year of the {{ill|Keiun|en|Keiun|qid=Q428753|lt=Keiun era|1=Keiun}} ({{ill|AD 707|en|AD 707|qid=Q23510|lt=707|1=AD 707}}) on May 16, a certain Murakuni Rento-shimē (村国連等志売) of Mino Province bore triplets. In this way, matters and persons associated with the Murakuni Clan are concentrated in Mino Province, lending strong support to the theory linking the Murakuni Clan with Kagami District, Mino.
The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' attributes the surname {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|qid=Q2735611|lt=Ren|1=Muraji}} to Murakuni Otokoroe at the time of the Jinshin War, and subsequent historical texts consistently record the Murakuni Clan under the Ren surname. However, the Ren surname in the era of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}}—in the period immediately before and after the establishment of the "Eight Colors of Surnames" ({{ill|Yashoku no Uji|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashoku no Uji|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}})—differed greatly in status, and the Ren surname at the time of the Jinshin War would have been too exalted for a minor local clan. An article in the 1st year of the {{ill|Taiho|en|Taiho|qid=Q423370|lt=Daihō era|1=Taiho}} ({{ill|701|en|701|qid=Q23496|lt=701|1=701}}) in July, which lists heroes of the Jinshin War along with their surnames, records Murakuni Otokoroe (referred to as Murakuni Koyori (小依)) without any surname. If this is given due weight, it suggests that the clan did not originally possess a surname at the time of the Jinshin War, and that the Ren surname was conferred upon them afterward.
== The Murakuni Clan in the Imperial Court == The Murakuni Clan is introduced in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' through Murakuni Otokoroe, who served {{ill|Prince Ōama (later Emperor Tenmu)|ja|大海人皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama (later Emperor Tenmu)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}} ({{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}}). In the year of the Jinshin War ({{ill|672|en|672|qid=Q30601|lt=672|1=672}}), in June, Otokoroe was dispatched as an envoy to Mino Province to deliver Prince Ōama's order to raise an army—likely a consideration of his Mino origins. In July, he served as a central commander of the forces in the Omi region, which became the main theater of operations. Following the Jinshin War, Otokoroe was granted a fudo (封戸). After his death, he was awarded the Rank of external {{ill|Koshiki|ja|小紫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koshiki|1=Shōshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11463467}}, and his son, {{ill|Murakuni Shigamaro|ja|村国志我麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shigamaro|1=Murakuni no Shigamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11523052}}, was granted a honor known as {{ill|Kōden|ja|功田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōden|1=kouden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11398438}}. Although these honors may seem modest compared to those given to the greatest achievers, they were significant rewards for someone originating from a local magnate.
In the first half of the 8th century, the Murakuni Clan attained mid- to lower-level noble status; Shigamaro reached the Rank of {{ill|Ju-go-i Jō|ja|従五位上|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ju-go-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, while {{ill|Murakuni Komushi|ja|村国子虫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Komushi|1=Q11523050|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11523050}} attained the Rank of external {{ill|Ju-go-i Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ju-go-i Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}}, barely reaching the 5th Rank despite his external status.
At the height of {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro|qid=Q540305|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro}}’s power, Otokoroe’s grandson, {{ill|Murakuni Shimasu|ja|村国島主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Shimasu|1=Q11523049|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11523049}}, initially entered Nakamaro’s service, and the clan enjoyed his patronage. Just before the {{ill|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|en|Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|qid=Q11623017|lt=Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion|1=Fujiwara no Nakamaro Rebellion}} in the 8th year of the {{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|en|Tenpyō-hōji|qid=Q1346307|lt=Tenpyō-hōji|1=Tenpyō-hōji}} ({{ill|764|en|764|qid=Q31163|lt=764|1=764}}), Shimasu held the position of {{ill|Shōyaku|ja|少掾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōyaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 掾|1=jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65269195}} in Mino, {{ill|Murakuni Koro|ja|村国子老|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Koro|1=Q11523048|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11523048}} served as the governor ({{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|qid=Q1351234|lt=Kokushi|1=Kokushi (official)}}) of {{ill|Noto Province|en|Noto Province|qid=Q1047671|lt=Noto Province|1=Noto Province}}, and {{ill|Murakuni Mushi-maro|ja|村国虫麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Mushi-maro|1=Q11523056|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11523056}} held the post of secretary ({{ill|Kaisei|ja|介|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaisei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国司|1=Kokushi (official)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1351234}}) in {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|qid=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province|1=Echizen Province}}. This distribution of posts appears to have been part of Nakamaro’s plan to mobilize military forces from eastern and northern regions centered on {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|qid=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}}, but his failure to raise an army and subsequent defeat meant that the plan never came to fruition. Shimasu was killed by an envoy sent to secure the fortification at {{ill|Fuwa Barrier|ja|不破関|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuwa Barrier|1=Fuwa no Seki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10868912}}, and the other two lost their gubernatorial appointments. Later, the Imperial Court exonerated Murakuni Shimasu. Although Koro and Mushi-maro, who had once fallen from power, later returned to serve at the same Rank as before, their descendants were unable to maintain their status.
Much later, a woman named {{ill|Murakuni Kazuko|ja|村国数子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Kazuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who worked in the Imperial harem, reached the Rank of external {{ill|Ju-go-i Ge|ja|従五位下|1=Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ju-go-i Ge|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fifth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従五位}} in the 17th year of the {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|qid=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan|1=Zhenguan}} era ({{ill|875|en|875|qid=Q23816|lt=875|1=875}}); she was the last member of the Murakuni Clan to attain the 5th Rank. Subsequently, the names of {{ill|Murakuni Gyōse|ja|村国業世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Gyōse|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (of the upper {{ill|Shōroku|ja|正六位|1=Senior Sixth Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Sixth Rank|qq=}}) and his son {{ill|Murakuni Harusawa|ja|村国春沢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakuni Harusawa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} appear in the "Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku" in the 4th clause of the 1st year of the {{ill|Ninna|en|Ninna|qid=Q1346317|lt=Ninwa|1=Ninna}} era ({{ill|885|en|885|qid=Q24894|lt=885|1=885}}).
